comment_text.sn12.16:2.1: In jarāmaraṇassa ce bhikkhu, bhikkhu can be read as either vocative or nominative. At SN 12.67:9.1 we find jarāmaraṇassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhu, where āvuso is the vocative and bhikkhu is nominative. If bhikkhu was the vocative here, it would result in a repeated bhikkhu bhikkhu. I suspect one bhikkhu was dropped in error to avoid this, so translate as nominative.

root_text.sn12.16:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.16:1.4: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, dhammakathiko hotī”ti?
root_text.sn12.16:2.1: “Jarāmaraṇassa ce bhikkhu nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya dhammaṁ deseti, ‘dhammakathiko bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya.
root_text.sn12.16:2.2: Jarāmaraṇassa ce bhikkhu nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti, ‘dhammānudhammappaṭipanno bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya.
root_text.sn12.16:2.3: Jarāmaraṇassa ce bhikkhu nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādāvimutto hoti, ‘diṭṭhadhammanibbānappatto bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya.
root_text.sn12.16:3.11: avijjāya ce bhikkhu nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya dhammaṁ deseti, ‘dhammakathiko bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya.
root_text.sn12.16:3.12: Avijjāya ce bhikkhu nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti, ‘dhammānudhammappaṭipanno bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya.
root_text.sn12.16:3.13: Avijjāya ce bhikkhu nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādāvimutto hoti, ‘diṭṭhadhammanibbānappatto bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn12.16:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn12.16:1.4: How is a Dhamma speaker defined?”
translation_text.sn12.16:2.1: “If a mendicant teaches Dhamma for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding old age and death, they’re qualified to be called ‘a mendicant who speaks on Dhamma’.
translation_text.sn12.16:2.2: If they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding old age and death, they’re qualified to be called ‘a mendicant who practices in line with the teaching’.
translation_text.sn12.16:2.3: If they’re freed by not grasping, by disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding old age and death, they’re qualified to be called ‘a mendicant who has attained extinguishment in this very life’.
translation_text.sn12.16:3.1: If a mendicant teaches Dhamma for disillusionment regarding rebirth …
translation_text.sn12.16:3.11: If a mendicant teaches Dhamma for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding ignorance, they’re qualified to be called ‘a mendicant who speaks on Dhamma’.
translation_text.sn12.16:3.12: If they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding ignorance, they’re qualified to be called ‘a mendicant who practices in line with the teaching’.
translation_text.sn12.16:3.13: If they’re freed by not grasping, by disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding ignorance, they’re qualified to be called ‘a mendicant who has attained extinguishment in this very life’.”

reference_text.sn12.16:1.1: dr16.23, ms12S2_92, msdiv16, ndp13.18, pts-vp-pli2.18, sya16.22, vri24.18
